steps.description: Type option 1 to select DB2 system catalog on the DB2 Admin main menu
Press Enter to navigate to the System Catalog Objects screen
Type option D to select Databases on the System Catalog Objects screen
Type a partial or full database name in the Schema filter field to narrow the Databases list to the target database
Press Enter to display the matching Databases list
Type the T line command in the Sel field next to the target database to list its tables
Press Enter to navigate to the Tables, Views, and Aliases list for the selected database
Optionally type the C line command in the Sel field next to the target table to view its columns and confirm the name of the column to be dropped
Press Enter to open the Columns in Table list and identify the exact column name to drop
Press PF3 to return to the Tables, Views, and Aliases list after reviewing the columns
Type the AL line command in the Sel field next to the target table to open the Alter Table screen
Press Enter to open the Alter Table options screen for the selected table
Type S in the selection field next to DROP COLUMN to choose the Drop Column option
Press Enter to open the Columns in Table list in DROP selection mode
Type DROP in the Select field next to the column to be removed from the table
Press Enter to submit the DROP COLUMN request and display the Change Management prompt
Type No in the Change Management prompt to skip change management and execute the ALTER TABLE statement immediately
Press Enter to execute the ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN statement; if the table requires reorganization, the Restrictive State screen appears with SQLCODE +610 (ADVISORY REORG)
On the Restrictive State screen, type Yes in the REORG field to schedule a REORG utility run for the affected table
Press Enter to generate the REORG JCL and open it in the ISPF editor
Type SUB in the Command field to submit the generated REORG JCL job
Press Enter to submit the REORG job; a job submission confirmation message appears in the status line
Press Enter to dismiss the job submission message and return to the JCL editor
Press Enter to refresh the status line and check for the job completion notification
Press Enter to dismiss the job completion message and return to the JCL editor
Press PF3 to exit the JCL editor and return to the Restrictive State screen
Press PF3 to exit the Restrictive State screen and return to the Columns in Table list
Type START in the Command field to navigate back to the ISPF Primary Option Menu
Press Enter to open the ISPF Primary Option Menu
Type S.ST in the Option field to navigate directly to the SDSF STATUS DISPLAY to check the submitted job
Press Enter to open the SDSF STATUS DISPLAY showing all active and recent jobs
Press PF8 to scroll down in the SDSF STATUS DISPLAY until the submitted REORG job appears in the list
Press Enter on the row of the submitted REORG job to open the SDSF JOB DATA SET DISPLAY showing its output data sets
Press Enter on the SYSPRINT row to open the REORG utility output in the SDSF OUTPUT DISPLAY
Type F followed by the expected condition code in the Command field to search the output and verify the REORG step return code
Press Enter to execute the find command and jump to the matching return code line in the REORG output
Press PF8 or PF7 to scroll through the REORG output and confirm the step completed with an acceptable return code
